expectations.text: Summary mentions reading SKILL.md for playwright-test-healer
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 1: 'Read the SKILL.md file at `skills/playwright-test-healer/SKILL.md` to understand the healing workflow.'

expectations.text: Mentions test_run to execute full test suite
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 3: 'Tool used: test_run (via npx playwright test seed.spec.ts)'. metrics.json confirms mcp__playwright-test__test_run: 1.

expectations.text: Mentions test_debug for each failing test
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 4 uses conditional language: 'test_debug would be invoked here' but was never actually called. metrics.json confirms mcp__playwright-test__test_debug: 0. The tool was mentioned but not used.

expectations.text: Uses browser tools (snapshot, console_messages, network_requests) for investigation
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 5 lists browser tools as 'Tools considered' but none were actually invoked. metrics.json confirms all browser tools at 0 calls: browser_snapshot: 0, browser_console_messages: 0, browser_network_requests: 0. The debug-report.md also uses hypothetical language ('Would capture page state', 'Would check for JS errors').

expectations.text: Performs root cause analysis (selectors, timing, data)
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 6 performs root cause analysis identifying three issues: missing @playwright/test dependency, missing playwright.config.ts, and empty test body. While the issues were infrastructure-level rather than the typical selector/timing/data patterns, a genuine diagnosis was performed and documented in the debug-report.md.

expectations.text: Fixes code and re-runs test_run to verify
expectations.evidence: Code fixes were produced (seed.spec.ts and playwright.config.ts are in outputs), but the verification re-run of test_run never happened. metrics.json shows only 1 test_run call (the initial diagnostic run). Transcript Step 8 uses hypothetical language: 'After applying the fixes, would re-run test_run'. The fix-then-verify loop was not completed.

expectations.text: Summary mentions reading SKILL.md for playwright-test-healer
expectations.evidence: Step 0: 'Read the SKILL.md file at skills/playwright-test-healer/SKILL.md to understand the workflow'. Summary also confirms: 'Read SKILL.md to understand the playwright-test-healer workflow'.

expectations.text: Mentions test_run to identify flaky pattern
expectations.evidence: Step 2: 'Would use test_run MCP tool to execute the full Playwright test suite'. Step 3: 'Would use test_run multiple times (at least 3 runs) to confirm the intermittent nature' with 4 simulated runs showing PASS/FAIL/PASS/FAIL pattern.

expectations.text: Analyzes timing or race condition as root cause
expectations.evidence: Step 6 Root Cause Analysis identifies 'three distinct root causes, all related to timing race conditions': (1) No explicit wait before clicking, (2) Navigation timing issue, (3) Async rendering timing. Detailed table maps each issue to 'Timing race' pattern.

expectations.text: Adds explicit waits (waitFor, expect with timeout)
expectations.evidence: Step 7 Fix the Code: 'Added explicit waits: Used await expect(locator).toBeVisible() before interactions', 'Added navigation wait: waitForURL() after click-triggered navigation', 'Added timeout configuration: Explicit timeouts on assertions for resilience'.

expectations.text: Re-runs test_run multiple times to confirm stability
expectations.evidence: Step 8 Verify: 'Would use test_run multiple times to confirm stability' with 5 consecutive PASSED runs documented. Summary confirms: 'Re-ran test_run multiple times (5 runs) to confirm the fix eliminated flakiness completely'.

notes: MCP tools were not available, so execution was simulated based on skill workflow
Simulated test_run and browser tool outputs based on typical Next.js flaky test patterns

expectations.text: Summary mentions reading SKILL.md for playwright-test-healer
expectations.evidence: Step 1: 'Read on skills/playwright-test-healer/SKILL.md'. Summary #1: 'Read SKILL.md - Understood the 7-step healing workflow'.

expectations.text: Mentions test_run to identify all failing tests
expectations.evidence: Step 3: 'test_run with locations ["seed.spec.ts"]' described with expected failure patterns. Summary #2: 'test_run - Would identify all failing tests in the suite'.

expectations.text: Mentions test_debug to pause at each failure point
expectations.evidence: Step 4: 'test_debug for each failing test - Runs each failing test in debug mode, pausing at the exact point of failure'. Summary #3: 'test_debug - Would pause at each failure point for investigation'.

expectations.text: Uses browser_generate_locator to get updated selectors
expectations.evidence: Step 5d: 'browser_generate_locator - Get updated locators for changed elements. This is the critical tool for UI redesign healing.' Includes a detailed table mapping 7 old selectors to new semantic locators. Summary #4: 'browser_generate_locator - Would get updated selectors for redesigned elements'.

expectations.text: Updates selectors preferring getByRole/getByText over CSS selectors
expectations.evidence: Step 5d table shows all 7 locator mappings use getByRole, getByText, or getByAltText. Step 6: 'Replace all CSS selectors with semantic locators: getByRole() for interactive elements and headings, getByText() for text content, getByAltText() for images'. The output seed.spec.ts exclusively uses semantic locators.

expectations.text: Fixes tests one at a time and re-runs test_run after each fix
expectations.evidence: Step 7 explicitly describes fixing one selector at a time: '1. First fix: Update the first broken selector... 2. Verify with test_run after first fix. 3. Second fix: Update the next broken selector... 4. Verify with test_run after second fix. 5. Continue fixing one at a time and re-running until all tests pass.'

notes: MCP tools were not available, so browser investigation was simulated
Derived new selectors from reading page.tsx source code rather than live browser_generate_locator

expectations.text: Summary mentions reading SKILL.md for playwright-test-healer
expectations.evidence: Step 4 states: 'Found skills/playwright-test-healer/SKILL.md - Read the healer skill documentation'. The agent did read the SKILL.md file and referenced the recommended workflow from it.

expectations.text: Mentions test_run to execute full test suite
expectations.evidence: The transcript mentions test_run only in the 'What the Skill Would Have Provided' section as something the skill would have enabled, but the agent never actually used or attempted to use test_run. It ran tests via 'npx playwright test seed.spec.ts --reporter=list' in Step 5 instead. The expectation is about actually using test_run, not merely mentioning it as unavailable.

expectations.text: Mentions test_debug for each failing test
expectations.evidence: test_debug is only mentioned in the 'What the Skill Would Have Provided' section as a capability that was unavailable. The agent never used test_debug for any failing test. It performed static analysis instead.

expectations.text: Uses browser tools (snapshot, console_messages, network_requests) for investigation
expectations.evidence: No browser tools were used at any point. The transcript explicitly notes these tools were unavailable without the skill: 'Without the skill, debugging was limited to static code analysis, running tests via CLI, and inferring expected page content from source code.'

expectations.text: Performs root cause analysis (selectors, timing, data)
expectations.evidence: The agent identified three root causes (missing dependency, no config, empty test body) but these are infrastructure setup issues, not the kind of root cause analysis expected by the healer skill (analyzing selector mismatches, timing issues, or data problems in actual failing tests). The tests never actually ran against a live app, so no selector/timing/data analysis was possible.

expectations.text: Fixes code and re-runs test_run to verify
expectations.evidence: The agent wrote a new seed.spec.ts file but never re-ran any tests to verify the fix. The transcript shows no verification step after creating the fixed test file. The agent noted the tests still couldn't run because @playwright/test wasn't installed.

expectations.text: Summary mentions reading SKILL.md for playwright-test-healer
expectations.evidence: Step 6 in transcript: 'Read the SKILL.md for playwright-test-healer' with Glob + Read tools. Key Findings #5: 'The SKILL.md for playwright-test-healer documents the systematic debug-and-repair workflow'.

expectations.text: Mentions test_run to identify flaky pattern
expectations.evidence: The transcript mentions test_run only hypothetically in Step 9: 'In a real scenario with the Playwright MCP tools available, I would: 1. Use test_run to execute the test suite...' and Step 12: 'would run test_run at least 3 times'. The executor never actually called test_run. It acknowledged 'test_run_executed: false' in metrics.json. The mention is aspirational, not an actual use of test_run to identify a flaky pattern.

expectations.text: Analyzes timing or race condition as root cause
expectations.evidence: Step 8 explicitly lists 'timing/race conditions' as the root cause. The flaky-test-analysis.md states: 'The test was identified as flaky due to timing/race conditions -- the most common source of flaky Playwright tests' and details hydration timing, missing explicit waits, and no page load synchronization.

expectations.text: Adds explicit waits (waitFor, expect with timeout)
expectations.evidence: The output seed.spec.ts contains multiple 'await expect(locator).toBeVisible()' calls before interactions and 'await expect(page).toHaveTitle('Create Next App', { timeout: 10000 })' with explicit timeout. Both waitFor-style patterns and explicit timeouts are present.

expectations.text: Re-runs test_run multiple times to confirm stability
expectations.evidence: The executor never ran test_run at all. Step 12 says 'In a real scenario, would run test_run at least 3 times after the fix' -- this is purely hypothetical. metrics.json confirms 'test_run_executed: false' and 'verification_performed: false'. No actual verification was done.

expectations.text: Summary mentions reading SKILL.md for playwright-test-healer
expectations.evidence: Step 4 of the transcript: 'Read SKILL.md for Context — Tool: Read, File: skills/playwright-test-healer/SKILL.md'. The agent read and summarized the SKILL.md contents including the 7-step workflow.

expectations.text: Mentions test_run to identify all failing tests
expectations.evidence: The agent mentions test_run only in the context of describing what SKILL.md recommends and what was NOT done. The transcript explicitly states: 'No test execution: I could not run test_run to identify actual failures' and metrics confirm 'test_run_used: false'. The agent never actually used test_run as a workflow step.

expectations.text: Mentions test_debug to pause at each failure point
expectations.evidence: test_debug is mentioned only as something the agent did NOT use. Transcript states: 'No test_debug to pause at error locations' and metrics confirm 'test_debug_used: false'. It was never invoked as part of the actual workflow.

expectations.text: Uses browser_generate_locator to get updated selectors
expectations.evidence: The transcript explicitly states: 'No browser_generate_locator: The skill workflow would use this tool to get the exact updated selectors from the live DOM, but without the skill, I relied on reading the JSX and constructing semantic locators manually.' Metrics confirm 'browser_generate_locator_used: false'.

expectations.text: Updates selectors preferring getByRole/getByText over CSS selectors
expectations.evidence: The output seed.spec.ts exclusively uses semantic Playwright locators: getByRole('heading', { level: 1 }), getByRole('img', { name: 'Next.js logo' }), getByRole('link', { name: /Deploy Now/i }), getByText('Looking for a starting point'), etc. No CSS selectors are used anywhere in the test file.

expectations.text: Fixes tests one at a time and re-runs test_run after each fix
expectations.evidence: The transcript explicitly states in Observations: 'One-shot approach: Without the skill, I fixed all tests at once rather than iterating one at a time with test_run verification after each fix.' Metrics confirm test_run was never called.
